basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: Muhammad — also called “the sūra of Fighting” (al-Qitāl) — Medinan, sets two destinies term against term. From the opening, God “brings to nothing the deeds” of those who deny and bar from His way, while He “sets right the inward state” of the believers and effaces their faults. It lays out the rulings of war — firmness before the enemy, favour or ransom “until war lays down its burdens” — and makes of the outward jihad, for Qushayrī, a mirror of the great struggle against the soul, which pours out its venom the moment one leaves it a remnant of life. At the sūra's heart stands the parable of the Garden — rivers of water never stale, of milk, of wine and of purified honey — set against the scalding water that rends the bowels of the deniers; in it Qushayrī reads the drinks of the saints here and now: fidelity, purity, allegiance, encounter. The call rings out: “if you support God, He will support you and make firm your feet,” with the warning not to weaken nor sue for peace when one has the upper hand. It is sealed upon the essential poverty of the servant before the absolute richness of God, and the threat of a people of replacement for whoever turns his back.
themes: the nullity of deeds and the setting-right of hearts
war and the great jihad against the soul
the parable of the Garden and the drinks of the saints
supporting God and the making-firm of the feet
the sealing of hearts and the disclosure of secrets
the poverty of the servant and the richness of God

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَصَدُّوا عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ أَضَلَّ أَعْمَالَهُمْ
versets.tr: alladhīna kafarū wa-ṣaddū ʿan sabīli llāhi aḍalla aʿmālahum
versets.trad: Those who disbelieve and bar others from God's way — God brings their deeds to nothing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: because they refused God's way and turned others from it, they earned to be veiled and held apart from the Presence. As for “bringing their deeds to nothing,” it means He annuls them utterly: disbelief miscarries works, just as faith delivers from perpetual punishment.

mots.racine: ك ف ر
mots.def: kafara: to refuse, deny; here to refuse the path.

mots.racine: ص د د / س ب ل
mots.def: ṣadda ʿan: to turn away, obstruct.

mots.racine: ض ل ل / ع م ل

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ وَآمَنُوا بِمَا نُزِّلَ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَهُوَ الْحَقُّ مِنْ رَبِّهِمْ كَفَّرَ عَنْهُمْ سَيِّئَاتِهِمْ وَأَصْلَحَ بَالَهُمْ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū ṣ-ṣāliḥāti wa-āmanū bimā nuzzila ʿalā muḥammadin wa-huwa l-ḥaqqu min rabbihim kaffara ʿanhum sayyiʾātihim wa-aṣlaḥa bālahum
versets.trad: But those who believe, who do righteous works, who believe in what has been sent down upon Muhammad — and it is the Truth from their Lord — He effaces their evil deeds and sets right their inward state.
versets.commentaire: Unlike the first, God sets these aright. Qushayrī: those who devoted themselves to God's obedience and committed nothing that opposes Him — He inevitably takes charge of the sufficiency of those who occupy themselves with Him. “He sets right their state” (aṣlaḥa bālahum): disbelief sinks works, faith frees from perpetual abode in the Fire.

mots.racine: أ م ن / ع م ل / ص ل ح
mots.sens: they believed and did righteous deeds

mots.racine: ن ز ل
mots.sens: in what was sent down upon Muhammad

mots.racine: ك ف ر / س و أ
ص ل ح / ب و ل
mots.sens: and set right their inward state
mots.def: bāl: disposition of soul, thought, state.

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا اتَّبَعُوا الْبَاطِلَ وَأَنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اتَّبَعُوا الْحَقَّ مِنْ رَبِّهِمْ كَذَٰلِكَ يَضْرِبُ اللَّهُ لِلنَّاسِ أَمْثَالَهُمْ
versets.tr: dhālika bi-anna lladhīna kafarū ttabaʿū l-bāṭila wa-anna lladhīna āmanū ttabaʿū l-ḥaqqa min rabbihim kadhālika yaḍribu llāhu li-n-nāsi amthālahum
versets.trad: That is because those who disbelieve follow falsehood, while those who believe follow the Truth from their Lord. Thus God sets forth for mankind their parables.
versets.commentaire: God strikes for the one their parables of good deeds, for the other their parables of misdeeds. Qushayrī: following the True is conformity to the Sunna, observance of God's rights, preferring His good pleasure, and standing in His obedience; following the false is innovation, acting by passion, preferring one's own portions, and falling into disobedience.

mots.racine: ت ب ع / ب ط ل
mots.def: bāṭil: the vain, the false, error.

mots.racine: ت ب ع / ح ق ق
ض ر ب / م ث ل
mots.sens: God sets forth for men their parables
mots.def: ḍaraba mathalan: to coin a parable.

versets.ar: فَإِذَا لَقِيتُمُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا فَضَرْبَ الرِّقَابِ حَتَّىٰ إِذَا أَثْخَنْتُمُوهُمْ فَشُدُّوا الْوَثَاقَ فَإِمَّا مَنًّا بَعْدُ وَإِمَّا فِدَاءً حَتَّىٰ تَضَعَ الْحَرْبُ أَوْزَارَهَا ذَٰلِكَ وَلَوْ يَشَاءُ اللَّهُ لَانْتَصَرَ مِنْهُمْ وَلَٰكِنْ لِيَبْلُوَ بَعْضَكُمْ بِبَعْضٍ وَالَّذِينَ قُتِلُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ فَلَنْ يُضِلَّ أَعْمَالَهُمْ
versets.tr: fa-idhā laqītumu lladhīna kafarū fa-ḍarba r-riqābi ḥattā idhā athkhantumūhum fa-shuddū l-wathāqa fa-immā mannan baʿdu wa-immā fidāʾan ḥattā taḍaʿa l-ḥarbu awzārahā dhālika wa-law yashāʾu llāhu la-ntaṣara minhum wa-lākin li-yabluwa baʿḍakum bi-baʿḍin wa-lladhīna qutilū fī sabīli llāhi fa-lan yuḍilla aʿmālahum
versets.trad: When you meet the disbelievers in battle, strike at their necks; then, when you have overcome them, bind them firmly; thereafter set them free as a favour or for a ransom, until war lays down its burdens. So it is: had God willed, He would have taken vengeance on them Himself, but He wills to test you one by another. And those slain in God's way — He will not let their deeds go to waste.
themes: the great struggle against the soul
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes the outward battle a mirror of the inward one. Once the enemy is vanquished, to hesitate and spare him out of excess clemency invites regret and squanders the chance: his roots must be uprooted. So too with the soul: when the servant has subdued it, he must leave it no remnant of life after its sting has risen, for whoever lays a finger on it, into him it pours its venom. Release or ransom hold good when the Muslims' benefit lies therein — a rest granted to the soul amid its toil, like an hour of sleep or the breaking of a fast, as the imam sees fit.

mots.racine: ل ق ي / ك ف ر
ض ر ب / ر ق ب
ث خ ن
mots.def: athkhana: to subdue by heavy losses.

mots.racine: ش د د / و ث ق
م ن ن / ف د ي
mots.def: mann: free grace; fidāʾ: exchange for ransom.

mots.racine: و ض ع / ح ر ب / و ز ر
ب ل و
mots.sens: to test you one by another

versets.ar: سَيَهْدِيهِمْ وَيُصْلِحُ بَالَهُمْ
versets.tr: sa-yahdīhim wa-yuṣliḥu bālahum
versets.trad: He will guide them and set right their inward state,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when one is slain in the way of the True, God takes charge of his heirs better than the slain one would have done himself. And more: He raises his degrees, magnifies his reward, and makes honourable his place of return.

mots.racine: ه د ي
ص ل ح / ب و ل

versets.ar: وَيُدْخِلُهُمُ الْجَنَّةَ عَرَّفَهَا لَهُمْ
versets.tr: wa-yudkhiluhumu l-jannata ʿarrafahā lahum
versets.trad: and admit them into the Garden He has made known to them.
versets.commentaire: The Garden is already known to them before they enter it: Qushayrī reads in “which He has made known to them” the promise of a familiar abode, as though the journey's end were but the recognition of what the heart already sensed. He raises their degrees and makes honourable their return.

mots.racine: د خ ل / ج ن ن
mots.sens: and admit them into the Garden

mots.racine: ع ر ف
mots.sens: which He has made known to them

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِنْ تَنْصُرُوا اللَّهَ يَنْصُرْكُمْ وَيُثَبِّتْ أَقْدَامَكُمْ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū in tanṣurū llāha yanṣurkum wa-yuthabbit aqdāmakum
versets.trad: O you who believe, if you support God, He will support you and make firm your feet.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the servant's support of God is the support of His religion by making the proof manifest and clarifying it; and God's support of the servant is the exalting of His word and the crushing of the religion's enemies through the blessing of his striving and resolve. “He will make firm your feet”: by the continuance of tawfīq, lest anyone be routed by the onslaught of the religion's foes.

mots.racine: ن ص ر
mots.def: naṣara: to aid, support, give victory.

mots.racine: ن ص ر
ث ب ت / ق د م

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا فَتَعْسًا لَهُمْ وَأَضَلَّ أَعْمَالَهُمْ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna kafarū fa-taʿsan lahum wa-aḍalla aʿmālahum
versets.trad: As for those who disbelieve — ruin to them, and He has made their deeds vain!
versets.commentaire: “Ruin to them” (taʿsan): Qushayrī glosses it as cursing and banishment, crushing and distancing. And “making their deeds vain” here means: tearing away their veils, disclosing their secrets to the believers, and quenching their fire.

mots.racine: ت ع س
ض ل ل / ع م ل
mots.sens: and He made their deeds vain

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ كَرِهُوا مَا أَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ فَأَحْبَطَ أَعْمَالَهُمْ
versets.tr: dhālika bi-annahum karihū mā anzala llāhu fa-aḥbaṭa aʿmālahum
versets.trad: That is because they loathed what God sent down, so He brought their deeds to nothing.
versets.commentaire: Their ruin comes from their loathing of what God sent down. Qushayrī: they hated the revelation because it exposed them — aversion to the True is never more than the fear of being laid bare. That is why their deeds are reduced to nothing (aḥbaṭa).

mots.racine: ك ر ه / ن ز ل
mots.sens: they loathed what God sent down

mots.racine: ح ب ط / ع م ل
mots.sens: so He made their deeds vain

versets.ar: أَفَلَمْ يَسِيرُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ فَيَنْظُرُوا كَيْفَ كَانَ عَاقِبَةُ الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ دَمَّرَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلِلْكَافِرِينَ أَمْثَالُهَا
versets.tr: a-fa-lam yasīrū fī l-arḍi fa-yanẓurū kayfa kāna ʿāqibatu lladhīna min qablihim dammara llāhu ʿalayhim wa-li-l-kāfirīna amthāluhā
versets.trad: Have they not journeyed through the land and seen what was the end of those before them? God destroyed them utterly, and the like awaits the disbelievers.
versets.commentaire: Travel upon the earth is an invitation to read the vestiges. Qushayrī sums up the fate of past nations in a single breath: how He annihilated them, exterminated them, swept them away. The same end lies in wait for whoever persists in denial.

mots.racine: س ي ر / أ ر ض
mots.sens: have they not journeyed the earth

mots.racine: ع ق ب / ق ب ل
mots.sens: the end of those before them

mots.racine: د م ر
mots.def: dammara: to destroy from the root.

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّ اللَّهَ مَوْلَى الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَأَنَّ الْكَافِرِينَ لَا مَوْلَىٰ لَهُمْ
versets.tr: dhālika bi-anna llāha mawlā lladhīna āmanū wa-anna l-kāfirīna lā mawlā lahum
versets.trad: That is because God is the Protector of those who believe, while the disbelievers have no protector.
versets.commentaire: Mawlā here means the Helper, but also, through walāya opposed to enmity, the Loving One: He is the mawlā of the believers, that is, the One who loves them, while the disbelievers — He does not love them. Qushayrī holds this verse to be the most hope-bearing in the whole Qurʾān: He did not say “the protector of the ascetics, the devout, the assiduous worshippers,” but “of those who have believed” — and the believer, though sinful, remains among those who believe, all the more since “they believed” is a verb, and the verb carries no absolute generality.

mots.racine: و ل ي / أ م ن
mots.sens: God is the protector of the believers

mots.racine: و ل ي

versets.ar: إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُدْخِلُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهَارُ وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا يَتَمَتَّعُونَ وَيَأْكُلُونَ كَمَا تَأْكُلُ الْأَنْعَامُ وَالنَّارُ مَثْوًى لَهُمْ
versets.tr: inna llāha yudkhilu lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū ṣ-ṣāliḥāti jannātin tajrī min taḥtihā l-anhāru wa-lladhīna kafarū yatamattaʿūna wa-yaʾkulūna kamā taʾkulu l-anʿāmu wa-n-nāru mathwan lahum
versets.trad: God will admit those who believe and do righteous works into Gardens beneath which rivers flow. But the disbelievers take their enjoyment and eat as the cattle eat, and the Fire shall be their abode.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī contrasts two ways of being in the world. Cattle eat anywhere, without distinction; such is the disbeliever, who makes no distinction between the lawful and the forbidden, eating at every hour without restraint. The believer is content with little: a third for food, a third for drink, a third for breath. And he adds: the beast eats in heedlessness; whoever eats while forgetting his Lord eats as the cattle eat.

mots.racine: ج ن ن / ج ر ي / ن ه ر
أ ك ل / ن ع م
mots.sens: they eat as the cattle eat

mots.racine: ن و ر / ث و ي
mots.sens: and the Fire is their abode

versets.ar: وَكَأَيِّنْ مِنْ قَرْيَةٍ هِيَ أَشَدُّ قُوَّةً مِنْ قَرْيَتِكَ الَّتِي أَخْرَجَتْكَ أَهْلَكْنَاهُمْ فَلَا نَاصِرَ لَهُمْ
versets.tr: wa-ka-ayyin min qaryatin hiya ashaddu quwwatan min qaryatika llatī akhrajatka ahlaknāhum fa-lā nāṣira lahum
versets.trad: How many a town stronger than your town which drove you out have We destroyed — and they had no helper.
versets.commentaire: An allusion to Mecca, the town that forced the Prophet into exile. Qushayrī recalls that this “We destroyed them” concerns the past generations swallowed up across the ages gone by: no force, no town, stands before the decree, and no helper rose for them.

mots.racine: ق ر ي
mots.def: ka-ayyin: how many, ever so many.

mots.racine: ش د د / ق و ي
ه ل ك / ن ص ر
mots.sens: We destroyed them, with no helper

versets.ar: أَفَمَنْ كَانَ عَلَىٰ بَيِّنَةٍ مِنْ رَبِّهِ كَمَنْ زُيِّنَ لَهُ سُوءُ عَمَلِهِ وَاتَّبَعُوا أَهْوَاءَهُمْ
versets.tr: a-fa-man kāna ʿalā bayyinatin min rabbihi ka-man zuyyina lahu sūʾu ʿamalihi wa-ttabaʿū ahwāʾahum
versets.trad: Is one who stands upon clear evidence from his Lord like one to whom the evil of his deed is made fair-seeming, and who follow their own passions?
versets.commentaire: “The clear evidence” (bayyina) is light and proof. Qushayrī distinguishes two clarities here: the learned stand in the light of their demonstration, the knowers in the light of their exposition; the first see by the rulings of the foundational proofs, the second by inspiration and union. One sees by demonstrative proof, the other is made clairvoyant by ilhām and wuṣūl.

mots.racine: ب ي ن / ر ب ب
mots.sens: upon clear evidence from his Lord

mots.racine: ز ي ن / س و أ / ع م ل
mots.sens: the evil of his deed made fair to him
mots.def: zuyyina: to be adorned, decked out.

mots.racine: ت ب ع / ه و ي

versets.ar: مَثَلُ الْجَنَّةِ الَّتِي وُعِدَ الْمُتَّقُونَ فِيهَا أَنْهَارٌ مِنْ مَاءٍ غَيْرِ آسِنٍ وَأَنْهَارٌ مِنْ لَبَنٍ لَمْ يَتَغَيَّرْ طَعْمُهُ وَأَنْهَارٌ مِنْ خَمْرٍ لَذَّةٍ لِلشَّارِبِينَ وَأَنْهَارٌ مِنْ عَسَلٍ مُصَفًّى وَلَهُمْ فِيهَا مِنْ كُلِّ الثَّمَرَاتِ وَمَغْفِرَةٌ مِنْ رَبِّهِمْ كَمَنْ هُوَ خَالِدٌ فِي النَّارِ وَسُقُوا مَاءً حَمِيمًا فَقَطَّعَ أَمْعَاءَهُمْ
versets.tr: mathalu l-jannati llatī wuʿida l-muttaqūna fīhā anhārun min māʾin ghayri āsinin wa-anhārun min labanin lam yataghayyar ṭaʿmuhu wa-anhārun min khamrin ladhdhatin li-sh-shāribīna wa-anhārun min ʿasalin muṣaffan wa-lahum fīhā min kulli th-thamarāti wa-maghfiratun min rabbihim ka-man huwa khālidun fī n-nāri wa-suqū māʾan ḥamīman fa-qaṭṭaʿa amʿāʾahum
versets.trad: The likeness of the Garden promised to the godfearing: therein are rivers of water never stale, rivers of milk whose taste never alters, rivers of wine delicious to the drinkers, and rivers of purified honey; and therein they have all kinds of fruits, and forgiveness from their Lord. Are they like one who abides forever in the Fire and is given to drink scalding water that rends their bowels?
versets.commentaire: Upon the four rivers Qushayrī raises a mysticism of the saints' drinks, here and now: the drink of fidelity (wafāʾ), then of purity (ṣafāʾ), then of allegiance (walāʾ), then of the state of encounter (liqāʾ). Each drink has its work, its intoxication and its sobering: whoever sips the drink of fidelity looks at no one in the days of his absence from the beloved; whoever drinks the cup of purity is washed clean of all admixture, pure of his own self, freed of his claims; whoever drinks the cup of allegiance loses all rest and is never absent from his secret a single instant; and whoever drinks at the hour of encounter is forever intimate with His subsistence, asking nothing further, annihilated in His loftiness beneath the assaults of His majesty.

mots.racine: م و ه / أ س ن
ل ب ن / غ ي ر / ط ع م
خ م ر / ل ذ ذ / ش ر ب
ع س ل / ص ف و
س ق ي / ح م م
ق ط ع / م ع ي

versets.ar: وَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ يَسْتَمِعُ إِلَيْكَ حَتَّىٰ إِذَا خَرَجُوا مِنْ عِنْدِكَ قَالُوا لِلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْعِلْمَ مَاذَا قَالَ آنِفًا أُولَٰئِكَ الَّذِينَ طَبَعَ اللَّهُ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِمْ وَاتَّبَعُوا أَهْوَاءَهُمْ
versets.tr: wa-minhum man yastamiʿu ilayka ḥattā idhā kharajū min ʿindika qālū li-lladhīna ūtū l-ʿilma mādhā qāla ānifan ulāʾika lladhīna ṭabaʿa llāhu ʿalā qulūbihim wa-ttabaʿū ahwāʾahum
versets.trad: Among them are some who listen to you; but when they leave your presence they say to those given knowledge, “What did he say just now?” These are they whose hearts God has sealed, and who follow their own passions.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī identifies them with the hypocrites: they hated what God sent down, because it carried their unmasking. The seal set upon their hearts is but the consequence of that aversion: they listen without hearing, and their feigned question betrays a heart already sealed upon its passion.

mots.racine: س م ع
ق و ل / أ ن ف
mots.sens: what did he say just now?
mots.def: ānifan: just now, a moment ago.

mots.racine: ط ب ع / ق ل ب

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ اهْتَدَوْا زَادَهُمْ هُدًى وَآتَاهُمْ تَقْوَاهُمْ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna htadaw zādahum hudan wa-ātāhum taqwāhum
versets.trad: As for those who are guided, He increases them in guidance and grants them their godfearing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of this verse a ladder of ascent: they were guided by the kinds of striving (mujāhadāt), He increased them in guidance by the lights of contemplation (mushāhadāt); they were guided by pondering the proof, He increased them by the spirit of exposition; they were guided by the science of certainty (ʿilm al-yaqīn), He increased them by the truth of certainty (ḥaqq al-yaqīn); they were guided by the courtesies of intimate discourse, He increased them by deliverance and the raising of degrees.

mots.racine: ه د ي
ز ي د / ه د ي
أ ت ي / و ق ي

versets.ar: فَهَلْ يَنْظُرُونَ إِلَّا السَّاعَةَ أَنْ تَأْتِيَهُمْ بَغْتَةً فَقَدْ جَاءَ أَشْرَاطُهَا فَأَنَّىٰ لَهُمْ إِذَا جَاءَتْهُمْ ذِكْرَاهُمْ
versets.tr: fa-hal yanẓurūna illā s-sāʿata an taʾtiyahum baghtatan fa-qad jāʾa ashrāṭuhā fa-annā lahum idhā jāʾathum dhikrāhum
versets.trad: Do they await but the Hour, that it come upon them suddenly? Its tokens have already come; but how shall the reminder avail them when it has come upon them?
versets.commentaire: The Hour will break in “suddenly” (baghtatan); its forerunning signs have already come. Qushayrī underscores the futility of belated remembrance: when reality bursts forth, recollection no longer saves the one who neglected it while he could still act.

mots.racine: ن ظ ر / س و ع
mots.sens: do they await but the Hour

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ب غ ت
mots.sens: that it come on them suddenly

mots.racine: ج ي أ / ش ر ط

versets.ar: فَاعْلَمْ أَنَّهُ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَاسْتَغْفِرْ لِذَنْبِكَ وَلِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَالْمُؤْمِنَاتِ وَاللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ مُتَقَلَّبَكُمْ وَمَثْوَاكُمْ
versets.tr: fa-ʿlam annahu lā ilāha illā llāhu wa-staghfir li-dhanbika wa-li-l-muʾminīna wa-l-muʾmināti wa-llāhu yaʿlamu mutaqallabakum wa-mathwākum
versets.trad: Know, then, that there is no god but God, and ask forgiveness for your sin and for the believing men and women. God knows your going to and fro and your place of rest.
versets.commentaire: The Prophet already knew that “there is no god but God”: the command “know” enjoins steadfastness in that knowledge. Qushayrī reads in it the servant's ascent in knowledge: first he knows his Lord by proof and demonstration — an acquired knowledge, the foundation of all reasoned science; then his knowledge is strengthened by added exposition, while his awareness of self diminishes under the sway of God's remembrance upon the heart; until the state of contemplation, where knowledge becomes necessary, and his self-awareness almost vanishes — like one absent from himself, forgetful of himself. And he draws the image: one at the shore is gripped by the sight of the sea; embarked, the state grows stronger; and once submerged in it, he is aware of nothing but that in which he is absorbed. “Ask forgiveness for your sin”: once you know that you know, ask forgiveness for that very thing, for the True, in the majesty of His measure, none knows Him but He.

mots.ar: فَاعْلَمْ أَنَّهُ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ
mots.tr: fa-ʿlam annahu lā ilāha illā llāh
mots.racine: ع ل م / أ ل ه
mots.sens: know that there is no god but God

mots.racine: غ ف ر / ذ ن ب
ق ل ب / ث و ي
mots.sens: your comings and goings and your rest
mots.def: mutaqallab: place of activity; mathwā: place of rest.

versets.ar: وَيَقُولُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَوْلَا نُزِّلَتْ سُورَةٌ فَإِذَا أُنْزِلَتْ سُورَةٌ مُحْكَمَةٌ وَذُكِرَ فِيهَا الْقِتَالُ رَأَيْتَ الَّذِينَ فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ مَرَضٌ يَنْظُرُونَ إِلَيْكَ نَظَرَ الْمَغْشِيِّ عَلَيْهِ مِنَ الْمَوْتِ فَأَوْلَىٰ لَهُمْ
versets.tr: wa-yaqūlu lladhīna āmanū lawlā nuzzilat sūratun fa-idhā unzilat sūratun muḥkamatun wa-dhukira fīhā l-qitālu raʾayta lladhīna fī qulūbihim maraḍun yanẓurūna ilayka naẓara l-maghshiyyi ʿalayhi mina l-mawti fa-awlā lahum
versets.trad: Those who believe say, “If only a sūra were sent down!” Yet when a decisive sūra is sent down in which fighting is mentioned, you see those in whose hearts is sickness looking at you with the look of one swooning at the approach of death. Better for them
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī recalls the context: the believers' hearts grew straitened at the delay of revelation, and they longed for it to descend swiftly. But as soon as a “decisive” sūra (muḥkama) mentioned fighting, you saw the hypocrites loathe it, for combat weighed on them: then they were unmasked, and looked at the Prophet with utmost loathing. “Woe to them” (fa-awlā lahum) rings as a threat.

mots.racine: ن ز ل / س و ر
mots.sens: if only a sūra were sent down

mots.racine: ح ك م
ق ل ب / م ر ض
ن ظ ر / غ ش ي / م و ت
mots.sens: the look of one swooning at death

versets.ar: طَاعَةٌ وَقَوْلٌ مَعْرُوفٌ فَإِذَا عَزَمَ الْأَمْرُ فَلَوْ صَدَقُوا اللَّهَ لَكَانَ خَيْرًا لَهُمْ
versets.tr: ṭāʿatun wa-qawlun maʿrūfun fa-idhā ʿazama l-amru fa-law ṣadaqū llāha la-kāna khayran lahum
versets.trad: would be obedience and fitting speech. So when the matter is resolved, were they true to God it would be better for them.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: it would be fitter for them to practise an obedience toward God and His Messenger, and a fitting word by their answering the command to fight. And when “the matter is resolved” — that is, when fighting becomes serious and obligatory — sincerity and compliance are better for them than their lying, their hypocrisy and their holding back.

mots.racine: ط و ع / ق و ل / ع ر ف
ع ز م / أ م ر
mots.def: ʿazama l-amr: the affair is settled.

mots.racine: ص د ق

versets.ar: فَهَلْ عَسَيْتُمْ إِنْ تَوَلَّيْتُمْ أَنْ تُفْسِدُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ وَتُقَطِّعُوا أَرْحَامَكُمْ
versets.tr: fa-hal ʿasaytum in tawallaytum an tufsidū fī l-arḍi wa-tuqaṭṭiʿū arḥāmakum
versets.trad: If you turned away, would you then spread corruption on earth and sever your ties of kinship?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī glosses: perhaps, if you turn from faith in Muhammad and revert to what you were, you will spread corruption on earth, shed forbidden blood, sever your ties of kinship, and return to your age of ignorance. Turning from faith returns a man to the barbarity that preceded the light.

mots.racine: ع س ي
و ل ي
mots.def: tawallā: to turn away, turn one's back.

mots.racine: ف س د
ق ط ع / ر ح م

versets.ar: أُولَٰئِكَ الَّذِينَ لَعَنَهُمُ اللَّهُ فَأَصَمَّهُمْ وَأَعْمَىٰ أَبْصَارَهُمْ
versets.tr: ulāʾika lladhīna laʿanahumu llāhu fa-aṣammahum wa-aʿmā abṣārahum
versets.trad: These are the ones whom God has cursed, making them deaf and blinding their sight.
versets.commentaire: The deafness and blindness in question are not those of the senses. Qushayrī: He made them deaf to hearing the True and accepting it with their hearts, and blinded their inner sight (baṣāʾir). The curse is this veil cast over the perception of the hidden meaning.

mots.racine: ل ع ن
ص م م
ع م ي / ب ص ر

versets.ar: أَفَلَا يَتَدَبَّرُونَ الْقُرْآنَ أَمْ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبٍ أَقْفَالُهَا
versets.tr: a-fa-lā yatadabbarūna l-qurʾāna am ʿalā qulūbin aqfāluhā
versets.trad: Will they not ponder the Qurʾān, or are there locks upon their hearts?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: were they to ponder the Qurʾān, it would lead them to gnosis (ʿirfān) and deliver them from the darkness of bewilderment. But the True has locked the disbelievers' hearts: a bolted door lets nothing in and nothing out; so too their hearts — the disbelief within does not leave, and the faith to which they are called does not enter.

mots.racine: د ب ر / ق ر أ
mots.sens: will they not ponder the Qurʾān

mots.racine: ق ل ب / ق ف ل

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ ارْتَدُّوا عَلَىٰ أَدْبَارِهِمْ مِنْ بَعْدِ مَا تَبَيَّنَ لَهُمُ الْهُدَى الشَّيْطَانُ سَوَّلَ لَهُمْ وَأَمْلَىٰ لَهُمْ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna rtaddū ʿalā adbārihim min baʿdi mā tabayyana lahumu l-hudā sh-shayṭānu sawwala lahum wa-amlā lahum
versets.trad: Those who turned back on their heels after the guidance had become clear to them — Satan seduced them and filled them with false hopes.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds a striking image of defection after illumination: one whose heart's dawn breaks, in whom the light of oneness glimmers, then, before the full clarity of his day, whose sun is eclipsed — the day of his gnosis darkens, the night of his doubt thickens, and the ardours of his intellect set… then tell of his darkness, and freely! He means those who enter upon the Sufi path, then undo their pact with God after traversing but a short distance.

mots.racine: ر د د / د ب ر
mots.sens: they turned back on their heels

mots.racine: ب ي ن / ه د ي
mots.sens: the guidance became clear to them

mots.racine: س و ل / م ل و
mots.sens: Satan seduced them and prolonged their illusion
mots.def: sawwala: to make evil seem fair; amlā: to grant respite/false hope.

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ قَالُوا لِلَّذِينَ كَرِهُوا مَا نَزَّلَ اللَّهُ سَنُطِيعُكُمْ فِي بَعْضِ الْأَمْرِ وَاللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ إِسْرَارَهُمْ
versets.tr: dhālika bi-annahum qālū li-lladhīna karihū mā nazzala llāhu sa-nuṭīʿukum fī baʿḍi l-amri wa-llāhu yaʿlamu isrārahum
versets.trad: That is because they said to those who loathed what God sent down, “We will obey you in part of the matter.” But God knows their secrets.
versets.commentaire: Their secret connivance with the enemies of revelation does not escape Him: God knows their secrets, their unspoken thoughts and concealed agreements. The whispered pact against the True is already inscribed in His knowledge.

mots.racine: ط و ع / أ م ر
mots.sens: we will obey you in part of the matter

mots.racine: ع ل م / س ر ر

versets.ar: فَكَيْفَ إِذَا تَوَفَّتْهُمُ الْمَلَائِكَةُ يَضْرِبُونَ وُجُوهَهُمْ وَأَدْبَارَهُمْ
versets.tr: fa-kayfa idhā tawaffathumu l-malāʾikatu yaḍribūna wujūhahum wa-adbārahum
versets.trad: How then will it be when the angels take their souls, striking their faces and their backs?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī lets the terrible scene of the soul's seizing resound: the angels take them striking their faces and their backs. The question “how will it be?” is less an inquiry than a warning of the instant when all concealment ceases.

mots.racine: و ف ي / م ل ك
mots.def: tawaffā: to gather up the soul.

mots.racine: ض ر ب / و ج ه / د ب ر

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمُ اتَّبَعُوا مَا أَسْخَطَ اللَّهَ وَكَرِهُوا رِضْوَانَهُ فَأَحْبَطَ أَعْمَالَهُمْ
versets.tr: dhālika bi-annahumu ttabaʿū mā askhaṭa llāha wa-karihū riḍwānahu fa-aḥbaṭa aʿmālahum
versets.trad: That is because they followed what angers God and loathed His good pleasure, so He brought their deeds to nothing.
versets.commentaire: Their loss lies in a twofold inversion: they followed what angers God and loathed His good pleasure. To prefer what stirs the divine wrath and to flee what draws His satisfaction is to condemn oneself to the miscarriage of every deed (aḥbaṭa).

mots.racine: ت ب ع / س خ ط
ك ر ه / ر ض و
ح ب ط / ع م ل

versets.ar: أَمْ حَسِبَ الَّذِينَ فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ مَرَضٌ أَنْ لَنْ يُخْرِجَ اللَّهُ أَضْغَانَهُمْ
versets.tr: am ḥasiba lladhīna fī qulūbihim maraḍun an lan yukhrija llāhu aḍghānahum
versets.trad: Do those in whose hearts is sickness suppose that God will not bring their rancour to light?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the matter is not as they imagine; rather, God will unmask them and disclose their dissimulation. And he recalls that the Prophet was informed of them: He apprised him of their very persons. The grudges (aḍghān) hidden deep in their breasts will be exposed.

mots.racine: ح س ب
خ ر ج / ض غ ن
mots.sens: God will not bring out their rancour

versets.ar: وَلَوْ نَشَاءُ لَأَرَيْنَاكَهُمْ فَلَعَرَفْتَهُمْ بِسِيمَاهُمْ وَلَتَعْرِفَنَّهُمْ فِي لَحْنِ الْقَوْلِ وَاللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ أَعْمَالَكُمْ
versets.tr: wa-law nashāʾu la-araynākahum fa-la-ʿaraftahum bi-sīmāhum wa-la-taʿrifannahum fī laḥni l-qawli wa-llāhu yaʿlamu aʿmālakum
versets.trad: Had We willed, We would have shown them to you, and you would know them by their marks; yet you will surely know them by the tenor of their speech. And God knows your deeds.
versets.commentaire: “By the tenor of their speech”: for the secret of the soul shows through upon the face. Qushayrī: the countenances reveal the inner depths, and what haunts the hearts, its trace gleams upon the features. Then he ranks the modes of seeing: the believer sees by the light of discernment (firāsa), the knower by the light of realization, and the one who unifies sees by God — then nothing can be veiled from him. And: the inner sights of the truthful are not veiled.

mots.racine: ر أ ي
mots.sens: We would have shown them to you

mots.racine: ع ر ف / س و م
mots.sens: you would know them by their marks

mots.racine: ل ح ن / ق و ل
mots.sens: in the tenor of their speech

versets.ar: وَلَنَبْلُوَنَّكُمْ حَتَّىٰ نَعْلَمَ الْمُجَاهِدِينَ مِنْكُمْ وَالصَّابِرِينَ وَنَبْلُوَ أَخْبَارَكُمْ
versets.tr: wa-la-nabluwannakum ḥattā naʿlama l-mujāhidīna minkum wa-ṣ-ṣābirīna wa-nabluwa akhbārakum
versets.trad: We shall surely test you until We know those among you who strive and who are patient, and test your tidings.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes the trial the touchstone of souls: by trial and testing the essences of men are revealed: the sincere appears, the lukewarm is unmasked, the hypocrite is exposed. Those who believed and purified their faith are saved and delivered; those who disbelieved and dissembled fall into humiliation and are abased, branded with wretchedness and cut off.

mots.racine: ب ل و
ج ه د / ص ب ر
mots.def: mujāhid: one who strives; ṣābir: one who endures.

mots.racine: ب ل و / خ ب ر
mots.sens: and test your tidings (your true state)

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَصَدُّوا عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ وَشَاقُّوا الرَّسُولَ مِنْ بَعْدِ مَا تَبَيَّنَ لَهُمُ الْهُدَىٰ لَنْ يَضُرُّوا اللَّهَ شَيْئًا وَسَيُحْبِطُ أَعْمَالَهُمْ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna kafarū wa-ṣaddū ʿan sabīli llāhi wa-shāqqū r-rasūla min baʿdi mā tabayyana lahumu l-hudā lan yaḍurrū llāha shayʾan wa-sa-yuḥbiṭu aʿmālahum
versets.trad: Those who disbelieve, bar others from God's way, and break with the Messenger after the guidance has become clear to them — they will not harm God in the least, and He will bring their deeds to nothing.
versets.commentaire: Those who rise against the Messenger after the clarity of guidance do God no injury: they will not harm Him in the least, their opposition diminishes neither His glory nor His sovereignty. The sole effect of their revolt is the miscarriage of their own deeds.

mots.racine: ش ق ق / ر س ل
ض ر ر
mots.sens: they will not harm God at all

mots.racine: ح ب ط / ع م ل
mots.sens: He will make their deeds vain

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَطِيعُوا اللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُوا الرَّسُولَ وَلَا تُبْطِلُوا أَعْمَالَكُمْ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū aṭīʿū llāha wa-aṭīʿū r-rasūla wa-lā tubṭilū aʿmālakum
versets.trad: O you who believe, obey God and obey the Messenger, and do not nullify your deeds!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī enumerates the ways of nullifying one's own deeds: do not annul them by ostentation, self-satisfaction and self-regard; nor by reposing upon them; nor by seeking some recompense in return; nor by the illusion that they earn you a due apart from God's sheer grace. The most upright deed collapses the moment one clings to it for oneself.

mots.racine: ط و ع / ر س ل
ب ط ل / ع م ل

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَصَدُّوا عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ ثُمَّ مَاتُوا وَهُمْ كُفَّارٌ فَلَنْ يَغْفِرَ اللَّهُ لَهُمْ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna kafarū wa-ṣaddū ʿan sabīli llāhi thumma mātū wa-hum kuffārun fa-lan yaghfira llāhu lahum
versets.trad: Those who disbelieve, bar others from God's way, then die as disbelievers — God will never forgive them.
versets.commentaire: What seals their fate is to die in disbelief. As long as life lasts, the door of return stays open; but whoever expires a denier after barring from the way shuts forgiveness against himself. The sentence targets obstinacy to the last breath.

mots.racine: م و ت / ك ف ر
غ ف ر

versets.ar: فَلَا تَهِنُوا وَتَدْعُوا إِلَى السَّلْمِ وَأَنْتُمُ الْأَعْلَوْنَ وَاللَّهُ مَعَكُمْ وَلَنْ يَتِرَكُمْ أَعْمَالَكُمْ
versets.tr: fa-lā tahinū wa-tadʿū ilā s-salmi wa-antumu l-aʿlawna wa-llāhu maʿakum wa-lan yatirakum aʿmālakum
versets.trad: So do not weaken and sue for peace while you have the upper hand and God is with you; He will not deprive you of your deeds.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī moves from the outward to the inward: do not incline to truce with the disbelievers while you are the higher by proof and by victory. Then he interiorizes: do not weaken in your hearts, and stand upright by God; for since God is with you, nothing of you escapes Him — He sees you unceasingly. And he concludes: whoever knows that his Master sees him bears every hardship, absorbed in His vision.

mots.racine: و ه ن
د ع و / س ل م
ع ل و
و ت ر / ع م ل
mots.sens: He will not deprive you of your deeds
mots.def: watara: to diminish, deprive of due.

versets.ar: إِنَّمَا الْحَيَاةُ الدُّنْيَا لَعِبٌ وَلَهْوٌ وَإِنْ تُؤْمِنُوا وَتَتَّقُوا يُؤْتِكُمْ أُجُورَكُمْ وَلَا يَسْأَلْكُمْ أَمْوَالَكُمْ
versets.tr: innamā l-ḥayātu d-dunyā laʿibun wa-lahwun wa-in tuʾminū wa-tattaqū yuʾtikum ujūrakum wa-lā yasʾalkum amwālakum
versets.trad: The life of this world is but play and diversion. If you believe and are godfearing, He will give you your rewards and will not ask you for your wealth.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gathers up the exhortation: shun associationism and acts of disobedience, that He may pay you your rewards. And he specifies the light measure of the due: God asks of your wealth only a tiny portion — the measure of the zakāt. The world is but play; what remains is faith and godfearing.

mots.racine: ح ي ي / ل ع ب / ل ه و
mots.sens: this world's life is but play and diversion

mots.racine: أ ت ي / أ ج ر
mots.sens: He will give you your rewards

mots.racine: س أ ل / م و ل
mots.sens: and will not ask you for your wealth

versets.ar: إِنْ يَسْأَلْكُمُوهَا فَيُحْفِكُمْ تَبْخَلُوا وَيُخْرِجْ أَضْغَانَكُمْ
versets.tr: in yasʾalkumūhā fa-yuḥfikum tabkhalū wa-yukhrij aḍghānakum
versets.trad: Were He to ask it of you and press you, you would be miserly, and He would bring out your rancour.
versets.commentaire: “To press” (iḥfāʾ) is to insist in the demand. Qushayrī specifies whom this warning addresses: this holds only for one not preserved from the avarice of his soul; but as for the brethren and those whose rank has risen in freedom of heart, they are not let off even an atom withheld: of them is demanded the giving of the soul and the acceptance of sacrifices.

mots.racine: س أ ل / ح ف و
mots.sens: were He to ask it pressingly

mots.racine: ب خ ل
خ ر ج / ض غ ن
mots.sens: and He would bring out your rancour

versets.ar: هَا أَنْتُمْ هَٰؤُلَاءِ تُدْعَوْنَ لِتُنْفِقُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ فَمِنْكُمْ مَنْ يَبْخَلُ وَمَنْ يَبْخَلْ فَإِنَّمَا يَبْخَلُ عَنْ نَفْسِهِ وَاللَّهُ الْغَنِيُّ وَأَنْتُمُ الْفُقَرَاءُ وَإِنْ تَتَوَلَّوْا يَسْتَبْدِلْ قَوْمًا غَيْرَكُمْ ثُمَّ لَا يَكُونُوا أَمْثَالَكُمْ
versets.tr: hā antum hāʾulāʾi tudʿawna li-tunfiqū fī sabīli llāhi fa-minkum man yabkhalu wa-man yabkhal fa-innamā yabkhalu ʿan nafsihi wa-llāhu l-ghaniyyu wa-antumu l-fuqarāʾu wa-in tatawallaw yastabdil qawman ghayrakum thumma lā yakūnū amthālakum
versets.trad: Here you are, called to spend in the way of God; yet among you are some who are miserly. Whoever is miserly is miserly only against himself. God is the Rich, and you are the poor. If you turn away, He will replace you with another people, and they will not be the likes of you.
versets.commentaire: Here Qushayrī founds a metaphysics of poverty: the miser harms none but himself, for he deprives himself of the only true gain. As for “God is the Rich, and you are the poor”: His richness is that His wills know no bound; the servant is poor by essence, for he never does without his Master, from the beginning of his creation to its end, and remains at every instant suspended upon Him. And he draws the definition of the true poor: the truthful poor one is he who witnesses his poverty toward God; whoever grows poor toward God is enriched by God, and whoever grows poor toward other than God falls into humiliation. As for the people of replacement, they would be more obedient to Him and more faithful, and would not be your likes in disobedience and turning away: rather, they would be better than you.

mots.racine: د ع و / ن ف ق / س ب ل
mots.sens: you are called to spend in God's way

mots.racine: ب خ ل / ن ف س
mots.sens: he is miserly only against himself
mots.def: bakhila: to withhold what is due.

mots.racine: غ ن ي / ف ق ر
mots.sens: God is the Rich, and you are the poor
mots.def: al-Ghaniyy: the Self-Sufficient; fuqarāʾ: the needy.

mots.racine: ب د ل / ق و م
mots.sens: He will replace you with another people
